name: Ari Atoll
description: Classic manta and whale shark season with drier weather and stronger visibility.

name: North Male Atoll
description: Reliable dry season diving with clear channels and consistent reef action.

name: Raja Ampat
description: Peak conditions for biodiversity, calmer seas and strong wide angle diving.

name: Komodo National Park
description: Long main season with good visibility, manta encounters and liveaboard conditions.

name: Tulamben
description: Popular wreck and macro season with calmer dry season conditions.

name: Bunaken
description: Best wall and turtle diving generally aligns with drier months and better visibility.

name: Malapascua
description: Prime thresher shark season with more stable seas outside typhoon peaks.

name: Tubbataha Reefs
description: Short liveaboard season when park access, sea state and visibility line up best.

name: Apo Reef
description: Dry season window generally offers better crossings and cleaner water.

name: Moalboal
description: Sardine run and reef diving usually shine during the drier part of the year.

name: Similan Islands
description: Main Andaman liveaboard season with calm seas and strong visibility.

name: Koh Tao
description: Best Gulf season usually avoids the stronger monsoon periods later in the year.

name: Richelieu Rock
description: Peak pelagic and whale shark timing usually falls in the open park season.

name: Sipadan
description: Strong visibility and schooling action typically improve through the drier half of the year.

name: Mabul
description: Macro diving is possible year round, but calmer conditions are common in the broad dry window.

name: Layang Layang
description: Seasonal remote diving with hammerhead potential during the calm weather operating window.

name: Mergui Archipelago
description: Best liveaboard season aligns with dry weather and calmer Andaman crossings.

name: Koh Rong
description: Local visibility and easier boat conditions typically improve through the dry season.

name: Nha Trang
description: Best visibility commonly falls outside the heavier rainy and storm season.

name: Green Island
description: Warmer months generally bring the most approachable conditions and visibility.

name: Yonaguni
description: Peak hammerhead season and strong current diving window.

name: Ogasawara
description: Warm season access lines up with dolphin, pelagic and blue water diving.

name: Jeju Island
description: Temperate diving is generally most comfortable and active during the warmer months.

name: Sanya
description: Warmer water and calmer weather usually dominate outside the coolest winter spells.

name: Havelock Island
description: Main Andaman dive season with the best mix of visibility and weather.

name: Bangaram
description: Best island conditions tend to fall in the dry northeast monsoon season.

name: Hikkaduwa
description: Southwest coast season usually peaks during the drier months.

name: Trincomalee
description: East coast conditions improve when the southwest monsoon shifts away.

name: Aqaba
description: Long comfortable season with warm water and easy shore diving access.

name: Daymaniyat Islands
description: Best reef and turtle diving generally falls outside the coolest and rougher periods.

name: Fujairah
description: More comfortable air temperatures and good boat conditions drive the main season.

name: Jeddah Reefs
description: Red Sea conditions are usually most comfortable outside the hottest summer stretch.

name: Brothers Islands
description: Prime offshore season for pelagics and stronger visibility on southern liveaboards.

name: Ras Mohammed
description: Long main season with easy access, reef life and warm water diving.

name: Shaab Rumi
description: Most liveaboard access favors the calmer, drier part of the year.

name: Dahlak Archipelago
description: Best access and heat avoidance usually fall in the cooler months.

name: Gulf of Tadjoura
description: Known for whale shark season and calmer conditions in late fall and winter.

name: Mnemba Atoll
description: Broad best window outside the strongest long rain period.

name: Watamu Marine Park
description: Warm clear water season usually falls between the big rainy periods.

name: Tofo
description: Cooler dry months are often favored for manta and whale shark encounters.

name: Memba Bay
description: Unstudied northern Mozambique coastline with seasonal whale shark and manta ray sightings, 1,000 km north of the well-known Tofo aggregation.

name: Aliwal Shoal
description: Main ragged tooth shark season and a favored cool water offshore window.

name: Sodwana Bay
description: Dry season months usually bring steadier ocean conditions and cleaner water.

name: Nosy Be
description: Broad main season with whale shark and reef diving improving after cyclone season.

name: Mahé
description: One of the calm inter monsoon windows with especially good visibility.

name: Praslin
description: Second inter monsoon visibility peak for granite and reef diving.

name: Mitsamiouli
description: Dry season months generally provide the best local access and visibility.

name: Sal
description: Spring to midsummer often offers good blue water conditions and marine life encounters.

name: São Tomé North Coast
description: Local diving usually improves in the drier months between rainy seasons.

name: Cozumel
description: Popular drift diving season with generally drier weather and good visibility.

name: Riviera Maya Cenotes
description: Cooler dry season months often bring especially clear freshwater conditions.

name: Socorro Islands
description: Main liveaboard season for giant mantas and offshore pelagics.

name: Silfra Fissure
description: Diving is possible year round, but the warmest travel season is the easiest planning window.

name: Great Blue Hole
description: Late spring often brings calm seas and strong visibility offshore.

name: Turneffe Atoll
description: Spring through early summer is a common favorite for weather and visibility.

name: Utila
description: Popular shoulder to dry season period with whale shark potential.

name: Roatán
description: Long favorable season with good wall and reef conditions.

name: Cocos Island
description: Green season window is favored for schooling hammerheads and large pelagics.

name: Catalina Islands
description: Dry season Pacific diving often brings easier surface conditions and mantas.

name: Coiba National Park
description: Main access season with calmer crossings and productive Pacific diving.

name: Bocas del Toro
description: One of the calmer Caribbean windows before stronger rainy stretches.

name: Malpelo Island
description: Prime offshore pelagic season with large shark schools and big animal action.

name: Providencia
description: Better weather and visibility generally fall before the late year hurricane season.

name: Wolf Island
description: Cooler current season is favored for whale sharks and schooling sharks.

name: Darwin Island
description: Iconic pelagic season with cooler water, current and major shark action.

name: Fernando de Noronha
description: Best visibility usually arrives in the drier second half of the year.

name: Arraial do Cabo
description: Cooler upwelling season is favored for visibility and marine life.

name: Los Roques
description: Dry months are generally favored for easy travel and clear reef conditions.

name: Bonaire National Marine Park
description: Famous year round shore diving destination with consistently accessible reefs.

name: Westpunt
description: Leeward Caribbean diving with broad year round reliability.

name: Tiger Beach
description: Main shark diving season outside the hottest summer stretch.

name: Exuma Cays
description: Favored liveaboard and shark diving season with calmer weather.

name: Bloody Bay Wall
description: Classic wall diving destination with broad year round reliability.

name: Stingray City
description: Accessible shallow diving and snorkel encounters throughout the year.

name: Saba Marine Park
description: Favored season outside the hurricane period for pinnacles and seamounts.

name: St. Eustatius Marine Park
description: Best weather usually falls outside the late summer storm season.

name: Bianca C Wreck
description: Dry season months are commonly preferred for wreck and reef combinations.

name: The Pit
description: Offshore conditions tend to be strongest in spring to early summer.

name: Poor Knights Islands
description: Late summer through autumn is a favored window for warmer water and visibility.

name: Milford Sound
description: Summer is the most practical season for access and weather in Fiordland.

name: Ningaloo Reef
description: Whale shark season and clear reef diving generally peak in late autumn to winter.

name: Julian Rocks
description: Warm water pelagics, leopard sharks and manta encounters build in summer.

name: Kimbe Bay
description: The drier season is typically preferred for reefs, wide angle and easier travel.

name: Milne Bay
description: Long broad season for muck, reef and liveaboard itineraries.

name: Beqa Lagoon
description: Dry season shark and reef diving is widely favored in Fiji.

name: Great White Wall
description: Soft coral wall diving is often especially good in the cooler dry season months.

name: Blue Corner
description: Peak visibility and dry season liveaboard conditions for current dives.

name: Rock Islands
description: Dry season conditions usually make inter island boat travel easier.

name: Manta Ridge
description: Main manta season with calmer trade wind conditions.

name: Chuuk Lagoon
description: Main wreck diving season with more settled weather.

name: SS President Coolidge
description: Dry season wreck diving window with clearer water and easier access.

name: Tiputa Pass
description: Long main season for pass diving, dolphins and big schools.

name: South Pass
description: Favored shark and spawning season for many advanced pass diving trips.

name: Bonegi Wrecks
description: Dry season visibility and logistics are generally strongest in this period.

name: Niue Reef
description: Winter dry season months are often preferred for visibility and cave entries.

name: Medes Islands
description: Warm season Mediterranean diving with marine reserve visibility and life.

name: La Restinga
description: Long Atlantic island season with stable weather and volcanic topography.

name: Vis Island
description: Best warm water Adriatic season for caves, walls and wrecks.

name: Gozo
description: Main season for archways, caverns and comfortable Mediterranean water temps.

name: Santa Maria
description: Warmest and most reliable offshore season for mobulas and blue water diving.

name: Capo Carbonara
description: Warm season Mediterranean visibility and reef life are best in summer and early fall.

name: Kornati National Park
description: Summer is the main boat diving season through the Kornati islands.

name: Florida Keys
description: Warm water reef season before winter cold fronts become more common.

name: Kona Coast
description: Warm season clear water diving with manta and lava topography highlights.

name: Channel Islands
description: Late summer and early fall usually bring the calmest conditions and best visibility.

name: Jardines de la Reina
description: Main liveaboard window outside the peak storm season.

name: Silver Bank
description: Peak humpback interaction season in the North Atlantic.

name: Anse Chastanet
description: Drier Caribbean season generally offers the easiest boat and shore conditions.

name: Speyside
description: Favored season for Tobago drift diving before wetter summer conditions.

name: Larnaca
description: Warm Mediterranean wreck season with calmest seas and best visibility from late spring through autumn.

name: Salt Island
description: Dry season Caribbean window for the BVI's protected wreck and reef diving outside the late summer hurricane months.

name: Great Barrier Reef
description: The world's largest coral reef system, stretching over 2,300km off Queensland. Back-to-back mass bleaching events in 2016, 2017, 2020, 2022, and 2024 have transformed large sections. Diving here documents what survives and where recovery is taking hold.

name: Malmok
description: Calm leeward bay off northwest Aruba sheltered from the trades, home to the WWII Antilla wreck and several smaller artificial reefs accessible by boat.

name: Scapa Flow
description: A wide, sheltered anchorage in Orkney, Scotland, holding the scuttled remains of the German High Seas Fleet from 1919 — the world's largest collection of divable WWI warships.

name: Tela
description: Coral cover above 60 percent when the Caribbean average has fallen below 20. Sea urchin populations here survived a 1983 pathogen that wiped out 95 percent of them everywhere else in the region. Scientists from the University of Miami, Operation Wallacea, and the Coral Reef Alliance are actively studying why — and early DNA work in 2024 found an unusually heat tolerant symbiotic algae inside Tela's elkhorn corals that is extremely rare elsewhere. Researchers are now crossbreeding Tela's elkhorn corals with Florida's nearly extinct populations to carry that resilience to reefs far beyond Honduras.

name: Wakatobi
description: A UNESCO Biosphere Reserve at the heart of the Coral Triangle, Wakatobi is one of Indonesia's most intact reef systems. Operation Wallacea has run continuous scientific monitoring here since 1998 and documented steady coral recovery following a 2010 bleaching event. Hard coral cover held at nearly 30 percent park-wide in 2019 surveys while destructive fishing declined. In 2024 when 84 percent of the world's reefs experienced bleaching-level heat stress, Wakatobi showed no significant bleaching — an anomaly researchers attribute to locally evolved heat tolerance in the coral colonies.

name: Sogod Bay
description: Sogod Bay hosts the second largest whale shark aggregation in Asia, with LAMAVE researchers having identified more than 350 individual sharks over a decade of monitoring. The outer reefs around Panaon Island reached 60 to 70 percent live coral cover in 2020 Oceana surveys — roughly three times the Philippine national average. Panaon Island was formally designated a Protected Seascape under national law in August 2025. Few dive operators work here and crowds are minimal even during peak whale shark season from November through April.

name: Derawan Archipelago
description: Derawan is one of the only places on Earth where reef manta rays are resident year-round. The Marine Megafauna Foundation has identified 155 individual mantas here — and 24 percent of the females have been observed pregnant, the only site in East Borneo where this is documented. With 500 plus coral species and 800 plus reef fish species the archipelago sits in the highest-diversity marine zone on the planet. The 285,549 hectare Berau MPA was declared in 2016 and active enforcement patrols with radar monitoring began in 2023.

name: Dahab
description: Dahab sits on the Sinai Peninsula where desert cliffs meet the electric blue Red Sea, and the diving here is legendary! The Blue Hole draws freedivers and scuba divers from every corner of the world, a cathedral sinkhole that plunges beyond 100 m alongside a coral wall of extraordinary beauty. The Bells and The Canyon offer adrenaline drift descents and sculpted swim-throughs that will stay with you long after you surface. Water temperatures are comfortable year round, and the town itself is one of the most relaxed and welcoming dive bases anywhere.

name: Sharm el Sheikh
description: Sharm el Sheikh anchors the southern tip of the Sinai Peninsula and has earned its place as one of the world's great dive destinations. Ras Mohammed National Park protects some of the most spectacular coral architecture in the Red Sea, and the WWII wreck of the SS Thistlegorm is widely regarded as the finest dive wreck on the planet. Expect schooling barracuda, resident sharks, and coral walls draped in soft corals of every color imaginable. The infrastructure here is world class, making it an ideal hub for liveaboard departures into the wider Red Sea.

name: Hurghada
description: Hurghada stretches along the Egyptian Red Sea coast and serves as the gateway to a remarkable collection of offshore reefs and historic wrecks. Giftun Island offers shallow coral gardens teeming with reef fish, while the notorious Abu Nuhas reef has claimed 4 ships whose rusting hulls now bristle with marine life. The reef at Shaab Abu Ramada is so rich with color and fish density that locals call it the Aquarium. Whether you are a first-time diver or an experienced wreck hunter, Hurghada delivers something extraordinary.

name: Pemba Island
description: An almost entirely undived island north of Zanzibar, ringed by walls that drop to beyond sport diving limits with zero crowds. Pemba sits in the Zanzibar Archipelago yet receives a fraction of its famous neighbour's dive traffic, which means healthy hard coral gardens, dense schools of fish, and encounters with large pelagics that feel genuinely wild. The steep walls are draped in gorgonians and black coral, and the currents that sweep the channels bring in barracuda, eagle rays, and occasional hammerhead sharks. This is frontier diving at its finest!

name: Puerto Galera
description: An easy day trip from Manila that punches above its weight with wrecks, walls, and world class macro in one sheltered bay on Mindoro's north coast.

name: Anilao
description: Batangas province's answer to Lembeh and considered the cradle of Philippine diving, Anilao's rocky reefs hold extraordinary nudibranch and macro density just 3 hours south of Manila.

name: El Nido, Palawan
description: Hidden inside Palawan's limestone karst wilderness, El Nido rewards explorers with pristine reefs, sea turtles, and manta encounters in the heart of the Coral Triangle.

name: Mauritius
description: Mauritius offers a surprising depth of diving beyond its beach reputation: an atmospheric WWII wreck, granite cathedral caves, and year-round warm water on the leeward west coast.

name: Réunion Island
description: A volcanic island with biodiverse reef systems regularly visited by humpback whales on their southern migration route, with unique coral diversity shaped by cold upwellings.

name: Kaş
description: A relaxed Lycian coast town where ancient history meets clear water: Bronze Age amphoras still visible on the seabed, dramatic canyon dive sites, and caves that cut through limestone cliffs into open sea.

name: Santorini
description: Below the famous caldera cliffs lies a unique volcanic underwater landscape with hot springs, pumice formations, and endemic species shaped by millennia of eruption cycles.

name: Lanzarote
description: Lanzarote hosts the world's first underwater sculpture museum and benefits from Atlantic upwellings that bring unexpected diversity to volcanic reef walls including angel sharks and rays year-round.

name: Okinawa
description: Japan's subtropical diving frontier with clear warm water on dramatic coral drop-offs and seasonal hammerhead schools that draw advanced divers to the outer reefs from spring through autumn.

name: Vava'u, Tonga
description: Tonga's Vava'u group is one of the only places on earth where humpback whales actively seek out encounters with in-water observers, turning July through October into something genuinely unlike anywhere else.

name: Rarotonga, Cook Islands
description: Remote Cook Islands diving where pristine outer reef walls drop steeply into deep blue water and passes fill seasonally with manta rays and grey reef sharks hunting in the current.

name: Turks and Caicos Islands
description: The wall dives here rank among the Caribbean's finest: the Providenciales reef edge drops vertically from 15 m to beyond 2000 m with crystalline 30 m visibility and regular pelagic flyovers.

name: Dominica
description: The Nature Isle of the Caribbean with the most dramatic underwater landscape in the Lesser Antilles: volcanic bubbles, black sand slopes, and the world's only resident sperm whale population accessible to divers.

name: Barbados
description: Barbados punches above its size with 6 accessible wrecks in a single bay and year-round sea turtle encounters on calm leeward reefs welcoming divers of every level.

name: Sea of Cortez
description: Jacques Cousteau called it the world's aquarium: the Sea of Cortez holds more marine species than anywhere else on earth and the only living coral reef in the entire Gulf of California.

name: Phuket
description: Thailand's dive capital with a fully protected marine park 20 minutes offshore and a year-round liveaboard departure point for the Andaman Sea.

name: Bali
description: Bali offers startling variety within one island: drift walls in the west, a WWII wreck in the north, and seasonal ocean sunfish encounters in the strait to the east.

name: Gili Islands
description: 3 tiny car-free islands off Lombok ringed by turtle-dense reefs and some of the friendliest shark dives in the region.

name: Lembeh Strait
description: The world capital of muck diving: a narrow strait in North Sulawesi where volcanic black sand conceals a rotating cast of rare and bizarre macro life found nowhere else in such density.
